name: CDC Small Business Finance
description_short: CDC Small Business Finance is a nonprofit SBA lender providing small business loans and SBA 504 commercial real estate financing with personalized advising since 1978.
description_long:
CDC Small Business Finance is a nonprofit Community Development Corporation that has been operating since 1978, making it one of the nation's established SBA lenders. The organization is now part of the Momentus Capital family of organizations, which focuses on mission-driven lending and community development. Over its history, CDC has provided $20.9 billion in small business financing to over 12,700 business owners while supporting the creation and preservation of 253,500 jobs.

The company offers two primary financing products: Small Business Loans (starting at Prime + 2.75%) and SBA 504 Commercial Real Estate Loans for purchasing buildings and equipment. SBA 504 loans are available in multiple term structures (10, 20, and 25-year options) with rates ranging from 5.310% to 5.786% depending on the loan type and term. CDC positions itself as more flexible than traditional banks, emphasizing personalized evaluation that looks at "your whole story, not just the numbers" rather than relying solely on credit scores and financial metrics.

What distinguishes CDC is its nonprofit structure, which allows it to operate without hidden fees and to offer extended advising services both before and after loan origination. The company provides business counseling and expert guidance to borrowers, positioning itself as a partner throughout the borrowing journey. They advertise the ability to prequalify borrowers for SBA 504 loans within 24 hours and offer business loan calculators to help borrowers understand payment obligations. The organization also actively engages in advocacy work, representing small business interests before Congress and relevant legislative committees.

As a nonprofit lender specializing in SBA products, CDC's primary limitation is that it focuses specifically on business lending rather than personal finance or consumer credit products. The rates listed are competitive but not necessarily the lowest available, and qualification typically requires a viable business plan and some creditworthiness. While the company emphasizes flexibility, SBA loans still involve standard underwriting processes and documentation requirements. The organization serves a specific niche—small business owners seeking capital for growth, real estate, or operations—and is not positioned for consumer lending.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Nonprofit structure means no hidden fees and more flexibility than traditional banks
Established lender since 1978 with $20.9 billion in cumulative small business financing
Personalized evaluation approach that considers full business story rather than just credit numbers
Offers both working capital loans (Prime + 2.75%) and SBA 504 commercial real estate financing
Pre and post-loan personal business advising included with loans
Fast prequalification for SBA 504 loans within 24 hours
Multiple loan term options (10, 20, 25 years) providing flexibility for different business needs
cons: SBA 504 rates change monthly and current rates (5.31%-5.79%) are not particularly competitive in historical context
Focus exclusively on business lending means not suitable for personal financial needs
SBA loans still require standard documentation and underwriting despite nonprofit status claiming flexibility
Limited information on approval rates, typical processing timelines, or specific credit score requirements
Service appears geographically limited despite national positioning (Momentus Capital family structure suggests regional operations)
best_for: Small business owners seeking SBA 504 financing to purchase commercial real estate or equipment
Startup entrepreneurs and established business owners needing working capital loans with personalized guidance
Nonprofit organizations and mission-driven businesses aligned with community development goals
Veteran business owners looking for affordable capital with supportive, relationship-based lending
diagnosis: CDC Small Business Finance is best suited for small business owners seeking SBA-backed financing for commercial real estate purchases, equipment, or working capital who value relationship-based lending and personalized business advising over the fastest possible approval. The primary caveat is that this is specialized business lending only—consumers need personal loans, credit repair, or emergency cash should look elsewhere, and even small business borrowers must meet SBA underwriting requirements despite the organization's emphasis on flexibility.
services: SBA 504 Commercial Real Estate Loans (10, 20, and 25-year terms)
Small Business Loans starting at Prime + 2.75%
Referral partner program for bankers and real estate brokers
Ongoing borrower support and customer service
SBA loan rate information and historical rate tracking
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 2448 Historic Decatur Rd STE 200, San Diego, CA 92106
